# AppA ADDRESSING Role: entry station for parsing, symbols, names, and canonical local/global addresses. Use when: - a new artifact enters the system - an address must be assigned - route computation begins

# AppB CANON Role: canonical law, normalization, equivalence budgets, and non-negotiable form constraints. Use when: - a claim needs normalization - two artifacts may be equivalent - form drift must be corrected

# AppC SQUARE KERNEL Role: discrete kernel pack for square-law interior organization and structured decomposition. Use when: - a chapter or appendix tile is built - discrete mixing or indexing is required - local structure must be stabilized

# AppD REGISTRY Role: registry of profile IDs, system constants, manuscript IDs, and pinned policies. Use when: - a new profile or manuscript family is registered - stable identifiers must be recorded - pinned settings change

# AppE CIRCLE GEAR Role: orbit law, phase order, cyclic progression, and chapter rotation machinery. Use when: - orbit successors are computed - cyclic order matters - rotation between arcs is required

# AppF TRANSPORT Role: transport, conjugacy, dual translation, and route legality between neighboring forms. Use when: - structure moves across a boundary - dual forms must be converted - a route needs a lawful bridge

# AppG TRIANGLE CONTROL Role: triadic control law, recursion steering, and lane-sensitive control semantics. Use when: - Sulfur, Mercury, or Salt rails diverge - recursion direction must be chosen - a lane-specific rule is needed

# AppH COUPLING Role: dependency closure, coupling topology, and construction-level adjacency. Use when: - two or more structures must bind - dependency graphs are built - construction integrity is checked

# AppI CORRIDORS Role: corridor truth, admissibility budgets, and abstain-over-guess discipline. Use when: - truth class must be assigned - a result is OK, NEAR, AMBIG, or FAIL - corridor budgets constrain interpretation

# AppJ RESIDUALS Role: residual ledgers, bounded error, and NEAR-state upgrade bookkeeping. Use when: - a result is close but not yet closed - approximation bounds must be tracked - an upgrade path to OK is planned

# AppK QUARANTINE Role: contradiction receipts, FAIL handling, quarantine packets, and revocation logic. Use when: - a route fails - contradiction is detected - a claim must be isolated from the active graph

# AppL EVIDENCE PROMOTION Role: evidence plans, AMBIG promotion harnesses, and proof-witness uplift. Use when: - evidence is incomplete - a candidate set must be preserved - missing proof material is being gathered

# AppM REPLAY Role: replay kernel, verifier discipline, deterministic re-checks, and certificate closure. Use when: - a claim must be replayed - a deterministic route is verified - a certificate is attached

# AppN CONTAINERS Role: containers, virtual mounts, salvage doctrine, and packaged runtime boundaries. Use when: - a bundle must be packaged - a sandbox or mount boundary is defined - salvage or extraction from a large archive is performed

# AppO PUBLICATION Role: export bundles, release signatures, and publish-ready packaging. Use when: - an artifact is released externally - publication intent is explicit - export signatures are attached

# AppP DEPLOYMENT Role: deployment profiles, runtime conformance, and monitoring-oriented release targets. Use when: - a system is pushed into active runtime - deployment profiles are selected - operational conformance is checked

heading_candidates: The project is not a single manuscript and not a single codebase. It is a compound organism with four main bodies:
records.excerpt: # Global Orchestration Synthesis The project is not a single manuscript and not a single codebase. It is a compound organism with four main bodies: 1. live manuscript execution (`Voynich`, `DEEPER CRYSTALIZATION`, `FRESH`) 2. formal and archive depth (`MATH` plus ZIP-backed frameworks) 3. runtime and retrieval (`self_actualize`, `Trading Bot`, `NERUAL NETWORK`) 4. governance and publication (`ECOSYSTEM`, `Athenachka Collective Books`) The live atlas now sees `6046` local records, while the archive atlas exposes `2041` additional hidden records. That means the active problem is no longer lack of material. The active problem is collision management, promotion discipline, and keeping the local and archive bodies in one shared route space. The strongest cross-synthesis is this: the repo alread
